Peer Review Status: Under Review...

A STUDY IN APPLIED INTELLIGENCE

d'agnelli, d. (2012-present) | london, uk

ABSTRACT

this paper presents the career trajectory of a practitioner operating at the intersection of artificial intelligence, machine learning, and enterprise programme management. the subject demonstrates sustained competency across supervised and unsupervised learning, deep neural networks, reinforcement learning, and large language models, while simultaneously directing programmes exceeding $15m in value.

KEY FINDINGS

the subject completed stanford machine learning (2016), followed by deeplearning.ai neural networks, udacity deep learning and deep reinforcement learning nanodegrees, and imperial college mathematics for ml. stanford probabilistic graphical models completed with honors. recent work includes genai rag systems deployment at enterprise scale.

METHODOLOGY

primary toolkit: python, java, go, rust. infrastructure: aws/azure/gcp, mlops, gitops. experimental design: agile & waterfall hybrid. sample sizes: 100+ plants, 2,000+ end users, 75k-employee organisations.

CONCLUSION

the evidence supports classification as a full-spectrum ai practitioner who chose the management track. gchq-certified msc in ethical hacking (distinction) noted as anomalous but consistent with subject's pattern of relentless acquisition.

CORRESPONDENCE: daniele@dagnelli.net
